generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Prioritize repairs that address unresolved identifiers or type mismatches reported by the AGREE validator.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Ensure correct data types (int, real, bool) are used in AGREE declarations and expressions.
Helper variables (eq) should be used to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to add architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing the contract itself.
Do not place guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
